title: Teaching
text:
I have taught in English and German across undergraduate, graduate, MBA, executive, and doctoral settings. My teaching combines research-informed content with application-oriented formats, including project-based learning, case work, entrepreneurial experimentation, and engagement with practice partners.



















Entrepreneurship und Gründungsmanagement (Lecture, Bachelor), SoSe 2026
Fallstudien im Gründungsmanagement (Seminar, Bachelor), SoSe 2026, WiSe 2025
PhD Seminar: Research Methods in Entrepreneurship and Innovation, WiSe 2026 (planned)





Crowdfunding and AI in Theory and Practice (Seminar, MSc), WiSe 2025/2026
Crowdfunding (Seminar, BSc), SoSe 2025
Grundlagen Wissenschaftliches Arbeitens (Seminar, BSc), SoSe 2025
Design Thinking – Project Course (Seminar, BSc), SoSe 2025, WiSe 2024
Consulting 1 – Project Course (Seminar, BSc), SoSe 2025, WiSe 2024
Consulting 2 – Project Course (Seminar, BSc), SoSe 2025, WiSe 2024
PhD Research Seminar (with Prof. Franke & Prof. Keinz), SoSe 2025, WiSe 2024





Entrepreneurship and Innovation – Navigating Uncertainty with Entrepreneurial Mastery (MBA), SoSe 2026
Entrepreneurship and Innovation: Navigating Uncertainty with Entrepreneurial Mastery (MBA), SoSe 2025
Entrepreneurship and Innovation (MBA), SoSe 2024
Entrepreneurship and Innovation – Theoretical Foundation and Practical Approaches (MBA), SoSe 2023
Digital Crowd Management (Master), SoSe 2022

Advanced Entrepreneurship (Master), SoSe 2017
Creativity and Entrepreneurship in Practice (Master), WiSe 2016
Introduction to Entrepreneurship (Master), WiSe 2016





AI-Driven Management of Sustainable Business (MSc / Programme Grande École), WiSe 2025





Digital Entrepreneurship (MSc), WiSe 2023





SolBridge International School of Business: Crowdfunding and Entrepreneurship; People and Organization / Leadership; Managing Digital Firms; Replicating Silicon Valley
University of Passau: Crowdfunding und Sustainability
Centre International de Formation Européenne (CiFE): supervision and evaluation of master’s theses
Jönköping International School of Business: Thesis Seminar (MSc)
Beijing Foreign Studies University: Innovation in Northeast Asia (Master)





My teaching is centered on entrepreneurship, innovation, digital transformation, entrepreneurial finance, sustainability, and AI. I place particular emphasis on project-based learning, transparent assessment, accessibility, and the integration of research methods and empirical evidence into class discussion. Across settings, I aim to prepare students not only for exams or final theses, but also for analytical and entrepreneurial decision-making in uncertain environments.

title: Andrew Isaak website package
text:
This is a Quarto website package prepared for GitHub Pages.



Upload all files in this folder to your GitHub repository andrewisaak.github.io
Install Quarto on your computer
In the website folder, run:
quarto render
Commit and push the generated docs/ folder
In GitHub:

Settings
Pages
Source: Deploy from branch
Branch: main
Folder: /docs






The visuals in images/ are clean placeholders so the site has a consistent visual structure immediately.
If you want to replace them with official institutional logos later, use the same filenames.
Add your CV PDF into the files/ folder and update links if needed.

title: Andrew Isaak
text:
Assistant Professor
University of Wuppertal
Visiting Professor of Business (2025–2026)
EM Strasbourg Business School




I am an academic researcher in entrepreneurship, innovation, and sustainable business, with a particular interest in digital entrepreneurship, artificial intelligence, entrepreneurial finance, and sustainability transitions. My work combines theory-driven empirical research with strong practical relevance.
My current main affiliation is with the University of Wuppertal. Previously, I held a recent appointment at WU Vienna and continued teaching there through March while also supervising bachelor’s and master’s theses. I have also taught at Mannheim Business School and served as a Visiting Scholar at the University of California, Berkeley.



Isaak, A., Istipliler, B., Bort, S., & Woywode, M.
Regulation, Corruption and Decentralized Autonomous Organizations: Insights from Bitcoin Trading and Platform Founding between 2011 and 2023.
Organization Science (forthcoming; ABS 4*, VHB A+)
Published article page




∼ €48.8k Fit for Funding Fellowship (VHB & Dr. Werner Jackstädt Foundation) for a project on AI and sustainability
Research Award, WU Vienna (2025)
PhD (Dr. rer. pol., magna cum laude), University of Mannheim
Visiting Scholar, University of California, Berkeley
Teaching experience in English and German
International teaching experience across Germany, Austria, France, South Korea, Sweden, China, and the UK
Research and reviewing activity spanning entrepreneurship, innovation, sustainability, and information systems





Digital Entrepreneurship
Artificial Intelligence and Organizations
Sustainable Innovation and Circular Economy
Entrepreneurial Finance and Crowdfunding
Regulation and Legitimacy in New Digital Markets




Email: isaak@wiwi.uni-wuppertal.de
ORCID: 0000-0001-5822-4355
Research · Teaching · German profile

title: Andrew Isaak – Deutsch
text:
Andrew Isaak
Ich bin (Senior) Postdoctoral Researcher & Lecturer an der Bergischen Universität Wuppertal (international äquivalent zu Assistant Professor).
Meine Forschung liegt an der Schnittstelle von Entrepreneurship, Innovation, Digitalisierung, nachhaltigen Geschäftsmodellen und Entrepreneurial Finance. Inhaltlich arbeite ich insbesondere zu digitalem Entrepreneurship, künstlicher Intelligenz, Crowdfunding, nachhaltiger Innovation und der Entstehung neuer digitaler Märkte.
Meine Publikationen erscheinen in internationalen Fachzeitschriften und Konferenzformaten, darunter Organization Science. Darüber hinaus verfüge ich über internationale Lehr- und Forschungserfahrung in Deutschland, Österreich, Frankreich, Südkorea, Schweden, China, Großbritannien und den USA.
Zur englischen Version

title: Research
text:
The list below follows a research-first structure for an academic job-market website: peer-reviewed journal articles, peer-reviewed conference proceedings, work under review and working papers, book chapters, and monographs.




Isaak, A., Istipliler, B., Bort, S., & Woywode, M. (forthcoming). Regulation, corruption and decentralized autonomous organizations: Insights from Bitcoin trading and platform founding between 2011 and 2023. Organization Science.



Lobo, R., Pigatto, M., Denes, D., & Isaak, A. (n.d.). The role of intermediaries in sociotechnical transitions: A systematic framework towards sustainability management. Business Strategy and the Environment, 1–21. https://doi.org/10.1002/bse.4237
Dinh, J., Yahyaoui, Y., & Isaak, A. (2024). Investing for good – uncovering crowd investors’ motivations to participate in sustainability-oriented crowdlending. Technological Forecasting and Social Change, 207, Article 123584, 1–15.
Dinh, J., Wehner, M., & Isaak, A. (2024). Sustainability-oriented crowdfunding: An integrative literature review. Journal of Cleaner Production, 446, Article 141579.
Theissen, M., Lutz, E., & Isaak, A. (2024). Family corporate venture capital: Syndicated investments from the perspective of socioemotional wealth and information asymmetry. Journal of Small Business Management (ToFE Special Issue), 1–38.
Ahrens, J.-P., Istipliler, B., Bort, S., & Isaak, A. (2023). How successor pre-succession firm experience affects post-succession performance in family firms. Journal of Business Research, 167, Article 114179.
Neuhaus, J., Bostandzic, D., & Isaak, A. (2021). Million dollar personality: A systematic literature review of personality in crowdfunding. Management Review Quarterly, 72, 309–345.
Schwieren, C., Iida, Y., & Isaak, A. (2022). Reaching agreement on contribution behavior in different cultures – a public goods game with representatives in Japan and Germany. Journal of Behavioral and Experimental Economics, 99, Article 101894.



Neuhaus, J., Bostandzic, D., & Isaak, A. (2024). When personality pays: Observer rating of personality and financial success of equity crowdfunded startups. Venture Capital, 26(1), 1–31.



Gruba, M., Denes, D., Lobo, R., & Isaak, A. (2022). Circular economy initiatives: Strategic implications, resource management, and entrepreneurial innovation in a Brazilian craft beer ecosystem during the COVID era. Sustainability, 14(19).




Zaggl, M., Steininger, D., & Isaak, A. (2025). Motivation by status vs reputation for voluntary contributions in online knowledge exchange communities. In Proceedings of the European Conference on Information Systems (ECIS 2025).
Koster, S., & Isaak, A. (2023). Giving neurotic entrepreneurs money to save the world? An analysis of U.S. equity crowdfunding projects. In Proceedings of the European Conference on Information Systems (ECIS 2023).
Bort, S., Woywode, M., & Isaak, A. (2021). Institutional legitimacy and new market emergence: The case of Bitcoin trading. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Information Systems (ICIS 2021).
Neuhaus, J., Bostandzic, D., & Isaak, A. (2021). Implicit personality and Big Five traits in equity crowdfunding. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Information Systems (ICIS 2021).
von Selasinsky, C., & Isaak, A. (2020). It’s all in the (sub-)title? Expanding signal evaluation in crowdfunding research. In Proceedings of the European Conference on Information Systems (ECIS 2020), Research-in-Progress Papers (Paper 56).
Ahrens, J.-P., Istipliler, B., Steininger, D., & Isaak, A. (2019). The Star Citizen phenomenon and the “ultimate dream management technique” in crowdfunding. In Proceedings of the International Conference on Information Systems (ICIS 2019).



Koziol, K., Bort, S., & Isaak, A. (under review). Investment risk disclosure: How platforms’ campaign ratings impact investors in equity crowdfunding. Submitted to Entrepreneurship Theory and Practice.
Ibrahimli, U., & Isaak, A. (under review). When technology meets purpose: Artificial intelligence, sustainability and startup funding in Europe. Submitted to HICCS Proceedings.
Göttel, V., Fahrenhorst, H., & Isaak, A. (revise and resubmit). Relationship marketing in the context of young enterprises: A systematic literature review and avenues for future research. Schmalenbach Journal of Business Research.



Sperber, S., & Isaak, A. (working paper). Illegal entrepreneurship and scalability. Accepted for the 2025 Babson Entrepreneurship Research Conference and a Research Policy special issue workshop.
Neuhaus, J., Bostandzic, D., & Isaak, A. (working paper). In the eye of the beholder: Personality signals and equity crowdfunding success. Best Paper Proceedings, Academy of Management.
Ahrens, J.-P., Istipliler, B., Steininger, D., Rottler, M., & Isaak, A. (working paper). Star Citizen: A social exchange theory perspective based on mixed methods.
Meyer, A., Weißenberger, B. E., & Isaak, A. (working paper). Re-examining control systems and pathways for transformational management by integrating sustainability into corporate investment decisions.
Steininger, D., Veit, D., Woywode, M., & Isaak, A. (working paper). Detecting deception in crowdfunding.



Townsend, D., Rady, J., & Isaak, A. (project in preparation). Sustainability and AI startups in Europe.
Lobo, R., & Isaak, A. (project in preparation). Technology forecasting and corporate strategy.
Lobo, R., & Isaak, A. (project in preparation). A case study on socio-cultural transitions and digital transformation in the Brazilian sustainable cosmetics industry.



Isaak, A., & Sauer, S. (forthcoming). Smart disclosure: How AI is reshaping sustainability reporting. In W. L. Filho (Ed.), AI and the green transition: European perspectives on sustainable innovation (World Sustainability Series). Springer Nature.
Volkmann, C., & Isaak, A. (forthcoming). Sustainable entrepreneurship. In E. Stam (Ed.), Handbook on sustainability management. Wiley.
Isaak, A. (forthcoming). Popular creativity techniques in innovation management. In B. Trusko & H. Croce (Eds.), Global innovation science handbook. McGraw Hill.
Koster, S., Isaak, A., & Lutz, E. (2026). The role of gender and sustainability in equity crowdfunding through the lens of social role theory. In D. Cumming, H. Hoque, & E. Kashefi-Pour (Eds.), Raising finance: From traditional to new forms of entrepreneurial finance. World Scientific Europe Ltd. https://doi.org/10.1142/9781800617032_0010
Isaak, A. (2025). Personality in crowdfunding. In D. Cumming & B. Hammer (Eds.), The Palgrave encyclopedia of private equity. Palgrave Macmillan, Cham. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-38738-9_215-1
Isaak, A., Bort, S., & Woywode, M. (2021). The geoeconomics of cryptocurrencies. In M. Munoz & M. Frenkel (Eds.), Economics of cryptocurrencies. Routledge, Abingdon, UK. https://doi.org/10.4324/9780429200427-15
Isaak, A. (2020). A resource-based perspective on refugee startup consulting: Lessons from German-speaking Europe. In M. L. Massimiliano, H. M., & M. L. (Eds.), The Entrepreneurship SIG at the European Academy of Management: New horizons with strong traditions (Vol. II). Information Age Publishing, Charlotte, NC. https://hdl.handle.net/10419/330216
Leicht, R., Berwing, S., & Isaak, A. (2019). How precarious is migrant self-employment in Germany? An analysis of national microcensus data. In W. C. & J. Schippers (Eds.), Self-employment as precarious work: A European perspective. Edward Elgar, Cheltenham, UK. https://doi.org/10.4337/9781788115032.00018
Isaak, A., & Liu, Y. (2016). Effectuation spectra in Chinese high-tech entrepreneurship: Domain-specific logic orientations and cross-border M&A. In S. Tarba & Y. Weber (Eds.), M&A on technology, innovation, entrepreneurship and competitive strategy (pp. 51–88). Emerald Publishing, Bingley, UK. https://doi.org/10.1108/S1479-067X20160000015006
Isaak, A., Isaak, R., & Zybura, J. (2016). Replicating Silicon Valley: Talent and techno-management in a culture of serendipity. In Y. Liu (Ed.), Entrepreneur and talent management from a global perspective – global returnees. Edward Elgar, Cheltenham, UK. https://doi.org/10.4337/9781783479139.00022



Isaak, A. (2019). Essays on trust in online peer-to-peer markets [Cumulative dissertation, University of Mannheim]. https://madoc.bib.uni-mannheim.de/46907/
Erlen, B., & Isaak, A. (2014). Business Ratios (BWL Kennzahlen). Wiley, Weinheim, Germany & New York, USA.




2026 Academy of Management Conference, Pennsylvania — Technology Forecasting and Corporate Strategy (poster session accepted)
2026 Nordic Conference on Small Business Research, Bodø — Technology Forecasting as a Strategic Tool: Implications for Readiness, Adoption and Sustainable Resource Management
2025 ZEW CODE, Mannheim — Measuring Sustainable Impact with AI
2025 Open User Innovation Conference, Lisbon — Measuring Sustainable Impact with AI
2025 Babson Entrepreneurship Research Conference — Can Illegal Entrepreneurship Contribute to Fairness? The Case of the Shadow Library Sci-Hub on the Distribution of Scientific Knowledge
2025 Academy of Management Conference — Investment Risk Disclosure: How Platforms’ (AI-based) Campaign Ratings Impact Investors in Equity Crowdfunding
2025 ECIS — Status and Reputation as Motivation in Online Knowledge Exchange Communities
